notes: Trigger-classification test set for skill-log-mining v0.1. should_trigger covers EN / JA / zh-TW phrasings of post-hoc JSONL transcript mining + MEMORY.md friction memo extraction + edit-proposal generation against existing dev-workflow:* / code-toolkit:* SKILL.md files. should_not_trigger covers adjacent-territory boundary cases (real-time coaching = claude-coach; new-skill discovery = crune; session-level = /insights; rubric scoring = skill-judge; output A/B = skill-tuning). Per memory feedback_run_loop_iteration_ties.md: do NOT iterate phrasing here to maximize run_loop scores when iterations tie — that's a framework limitation, not a description issue.
should_trigger: I've burned through a lot of code-toolkit PRs lately — what can I learn from the JSONL logs about which skills missed-trigger?
Mine my Claude Code session logs to find friction patterns in skill brief-before-asking and propose SKILL.md edits
Distill MEMORY.md friction memos into concrete SKILL.md edit proposals for dev-workflow skills
Analyze ~/.claude/projects/ JSONL transcripts to figure out which dev-workflow skills need iteration this week
技能ログから知見を蒸留して既存 SKILL.md の description / body 改善提案を出してほしい
ここ最近の PR から code-toolkit の活性化漏れパターンを見つけて skill を改善したい
從 JSONL transcript 挖掘 skill iteration 機會，產生 dev-workflow:* 的 SKILL.md edit proposal
把累積在 MEMORY.md 的 friction memo 整理成具體的 SKILL.md 修改提案
should_not_trigger: Coach me in real time while I'm coding — interrupt me when I'm about to make a mistake
Discover new skills I might want to create from clustering my recent prompts
Show me the /insights report for the last 30 days of skill activations
Score this SKILL.md against the 8-dimension skill-judge rubric and tell me where it loses points
Run an A/B test on three different phrasings of this skill description and let me pick which one feels best
